message:
fix(protocol): add length-prefix encoding to all cryptographic byte concatenation

Prevents canonicalization ambiguity attacks by prepending a 4-byte LE
u32 length before each variable-length field in HKDF info strings,
signing bytes, and hash inputs. Without length prefixes, different
field combinations can produce identical concatenated bytes (e.g.,
("ab","cd") vs ("abc","d") both yield "abcd"), enabling potential
signature/key confusion.

Affected protocol surfaces:
- FCP2-SESSION-V1: initiator_node_id + responder_node_id in HKDF info
- FCP2-SENDER-KEY-V1: sender_node_id in HKDF info (both symbol_envelope
  and raptorq envelope variants)
- FCP2-HRW-V1: zone_id + subject_id + node_id in BLAKE3 rendezvous hash
- FCP2-GOSSIP-SUMMARY-V1: from + zone_id + epoch_id in signing bytes
- FCP2-HOLDER-PROOF-V1: safe u32::try_from conversion for request_id,
  operation_id, and token_jti (replaces unchecked `as u32` cast)

Additional fixes applied during review:
- assert! -> assert_eq! in InvokeContext test (assert! with two args
  was silently using format-string semantics, not equality)
- .unwrap() -> .expect() in DegradedModeDecoder for descriptive panics
- shared.as_bytes() -> &shared type mismatch fix in session interop

All conformance golden vectors regenerated to match new encoding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
